title: Forward SYNCHRONIZE_PEER_ID in Claude+Pi session hooks
description: Enabler for daemon-pinned peer_id. src/cli/commands/hook.ts (runClaudeHook + runPiHook) must forward peerId to registerAgentSession when ENV_PEER_ID is set (no-op otherwise; register endpoint already accepts peer_id). Backward-safe. See docs/plans/aoe-agent-launch.md decision 7.
close_reason: Hooks forward SYNCHRONIZE_PEER_ID; typecheck clean, peer-id-env passes, no regression vs master baseline

title: AOE-backed agent launch (daemon-managed, group-aware) [v0]
description: Daemon can launch persistent Claude/Pi sessions via AOE as the tmux backend, optionally auto-joining a synchronize group. Surfaces: REST + CLI + MCP (no UI in v0). Design: docs/plans/aoe-agent-launch.md. Key decisions: server-side group join at register (reconcileLaunch); in-memory launch map (no pending_launches table); pin peer_id at launch; AOE title=session_name-peerid8; rely on global install; swappable SessionBackend (AoeBackend only now); zero new env vars, no DB migration.
close_reason: AOE-backed agent launch shipped to master (7a061d3): REST+CLI+MCP, server-side group auto-join, live channel push for spawned Claude (dev flag + auto-confirm + proactive MCP activation), --model haiku default. Full suite 105/0. Follow-ups: sync-14e (Pi parity), sync-zst (channel allowlist).

title: Switch web datasource to daemon-owned session
description: What/why/how: update the web DaemonDataSource startup/connect flow to call the daemon local web session endpoint and use the returned peer_id for /web/state, autojoin, group messages, DMs, room refreshes, and optimistic messages. Browser localStorage may remain for UI metadata but must not be the source of group participation identity. Impact area: web/src/data/daemon.ts and browser startup behavior. Whole-plan context: multiple tabs/profiles are controllers for one local human participant, so sending from any surface should render as the same visible 'you'.
acceptance_criteria:
- Multiple browser tabs/profiles converge on the same peer_id from the daemon.
- Web autojoin no longer creates one group member per browser profile.
- Sending group messages and DMs still works from the UI.
- Old localStorage peer IDs do not prevent daemon session identity from becoming source of truth.
notes: How to verify: manual browser test plus web typecheck. Test plan: web typecheck and targeted datasource tests if current setup supports them. Dependencies: sync-z2q.1. Improvements: later replace endpoint consumption with principal/session registration.
close_reason: Switched web DaemonDataSource to POST /web/session as source of participation identity; web build and web typecheck pass after adding missing bun-types dev dependency.

title: Add web-only stale you reclaim behavior
description: What/why/how: when the daemon-owned local web peer joins a group as alias 'you', deactivate/reclaim stale active 'you' memberships held by other peers where tool='web'. Preserve normal alias collision behavior for Claude/Codex/Pi and all other non-web participants. Impact area: group join path, alias collision handling, roster events, inbox fanout, and debugging state. Whole-plan context: this fixes existing broken DB state where an old web peer blocks the new local web session, without allowing duplicate active aliases.
acceptance_criteria:
- Old active web-held 'you' no longer blocks the daemon-owned local web peer.
- Non-web participants holding 'you' still cause alias_collision.
- After reclaim, there is only one active 'you' member for the group.
- Reclaim behavior is observable enough for debugging without weakening alias uniqueness.
notes: How to verify: seed a group with an old web 'you', join with the local web peer, and inspect active members. Test plan: daemon integration tests for web reclaim and non-web collision. Dependencies: sync-z2q.1. Improvements: later principal migration can replace web-specific reclaim with principal-owned membership.
close_reason: Implemented local web session reclaim for stale active web-held alias 'you' while preserving non-web alias collisions; verified with bun test tests/api.test.ts -t 'local web session'.

title: Add daemon local web session endpoint
description: What/why/how: add a daemon endpoint such as POST /web/session that returns a stable daemon-owned local web peer for the current SYNCHRONIZE_HOME. This is the first option-1 implementation slice: browser tabs/profiles must ask the daemon for the local human web participant instead of minting independent group identities. Impact area: daemon REST routing, peer registration/upsert behavior, web API contract, and tests around peer persistence. Whole-plan context: alias uniqueness stays intact; opening /web/ remains a zero-step localhost browser action; option 2 principal identity is deferred.
acceptance_criteria:
- Repeated endpoint calls return the same peer_id for the daemon home.
- Returned peer is registered as tool='web' and visible in web state.
- Default localhost usage requires no extra UI/auth steps beyond existing daemon auth behavior.
- Endpoint shape is documented enough for the web datasource issue to consume.
notes: How to verify: daemon integration test calls the endpoint twice and confirms stable peer identity. Test plan: Bun test around endpoint behavior and peer row persistence. Dependencies: none. Improvements: this can later become a local-principal endpoint when option 2 is implemented.
close_reason: Implemented POST /web/session returning stable daemon-owned local web peer web:local-human; verified with bun test tests/api.test.ts -t 'web session endpoint'.

title: Implement daemon-owned local web session store
description: Goal: ship the immediate option-1 fix so multiple local browser tabs and profiles can use the web UI, join groups as alias 'you', and send messages without alias collisions. Whole-plan context: the daemon becomes the local session store for the human web participant; group alias uniqueness remains intact; browser tabs/profiles are controllers for one daemon-owned local web peer; option 2 principal identity is deferred. Affected areas: daemon web/session API, web DaemonDataSource startup, group membership alias behavior, tests, docs, and synchronize-debugging skill references. Verify by opening /web/ from multiple tabs/profiles, sending messages as one visible 'you', and inspecting one active 'you' group member.
acceptance_criteria:
- Epic 1 child issues are complete.
- Multiple local browser tabs/profiles can send as you without alias collision.
- Roster shows one active you member per group.
- Existing non-web alias collision behavior is preserved.
- Tests/typechecks/manual verification are recorded.
close_reason: Completed option-1 daemon-owned local web session store. All child issues closed. Verified multi-tab/profile behavior by manual daemon calls and ran SYNCHRONIZE_PORT=0 bun test --timeout 15000, bun run typecheck, and web typecheck/build.

title: Allow multiple web observers to participate in groups
description: The web UI currently auto-joins every group as alias 'you', so a second browser observer hits alias_collision. We need web observers to remain able to send messages while avoiding collisions between multiple GUI sessions.

title: Pre-refactor: HTTP response snapshot fixture for every daemon endpoint
description:
Capture a golden-test fixture of the daemon's wire contract (status + JSON body shape) for every REST endpoint, recorded against the current pre-refactor daemon. The same fixture must replay byte-identically against the daemon after sync-mkj.10 and sync-mkj.11.

## Why
Integration tests assert specific fields they care about, but no test catches incidental shape drift — a renamed key, a flipped boolean encoding, a dropped field, a reordered array element. The refactor moves ~30 endpoints across ~12 new files. A snapshot fixture is the single strongest safety net for the HTTP wire contract (Layer 1 of the four daemon contracts in the design doc).

## Approach
- New test file tests/daemon-snapshots.test.ts.
- Seed a deterministic daemon state (fixed peer ids, group names, event ids by ordering inserts).
- For every endpoint in src/daemon.ts route(), call it and snapshot {status, normalized JSON body}.
- Normalize volatile fields (timestamps, hostnames, ports, pids, uuids) via a small replacer so the snapshot is stable across runs.
- Cover both happy path and at least one error-path snapshot per endpoint group (404, 403, 409 where applicable).

## In scope
- /health, /status, /summary
- /agent-sessions (register, list, rename, get)
- /peers (register, heartbeat, list, delete)
- /subscriptions
- /dm
- /groups (create, list, get, join, rename, patch, leave, messages, history, media)
- /events/:id, /events/:id/thread
- /media/:id
- /peers/:id/inbox, /peers/:id/inbox/ack, /peers/:id/events
- /web/state (SSE /web/events covered by sync-mkj.12; /web/* static assets snapshot is optional)

## Acceptance
- Fixture captures every endpoint listed above for at least one happy path + one error path.
- Snapshot replay passes on the current branch.
- Snapshot replay must pass unchanged after sync-mkj.10 and sync-mkj.11 land.
- bun run typecheck && bun test green.

## Verification
bun test tests/daemon-snapshots.test.ts

title: Pre-refactor: route precedence + validation + fallthrough regression tests
description:
Backfill test coverage at the seams the daemon split will cross, before any code in src/daemon.ts moves. See docs/daemon-modularization-plan.md for the precedence overlap table and the 5 blind spots.

## In scope
1. **Route-precedence pair assertions** — for each overlap in the design doc's §4 table, add one back-to-back assertion that both endpoints return their expected behavior:
   - GET /agent-sessions vs GET /agent-sessions/:id
   - GET /groups vs GET /groups/:name
   - GET /events/:id vs GET /events/:id/thread
   - PATCH /peers/:id/heartbeat vs DELETE /peers/:id
   - POST /groups vs POST /groups/:name/{join,rename,leave,messages,media}
2. **Validation unit tests** — new tests/daemon-validation.test.ts covering rejected inputs for requireString, optionalString, optionalInteger, optionalObjectJson, optionalNumberArray, requireLocalCallbackUrl, requireGroupName, parseLimit (limit=0, negative, non-numeric), parseCursor (cursor=-1), parseOptionalPositiveInt. These helpers currently only get happy-path coverage through integration tests.
3. **404 fallthrough** — assert unknown path returns {error: {code: 'not_found'}} with status 404.
4. **Subscriber removal on callback failure** — POST /subscriptions, then trigger an event whose callback returns 500 (or where the callback URL refuses); assert ctx.subscribers no longer contains the entry on subsequent attempts.
5. **SSE client cancel cleanup** — open /web/events, cancel the stream client-side, assert ctx.webStateClients does not retain the entry (verified via a subsequent state_changed not throwing on the dead client).

## Why
The current 47 tests assert end-state behavior of each endpoint in isolation, but no test pairs the overlapping endpoints together, no test exercises validation rejections directly, and the subscriber/SSE cleanup branches are not asserted. These are exactly the boundaries sync-mkj.10 and .11 will cross. Without backfill, the split could pass the existing suite while introducing precedence flips or silent cleanup leaks.

## Acceptance
- All 5 new test groups land and pass against the current (pre-refactor) daemon.
- bun test, bun run typecheck pass.
- These tests must continue to pass after sync-mkj.10 and sync-mkj.11.

## Verification
bun run typecheck && bun test

title: Structured error envelope across all tool/HTTP errors
description: Currently rename collisions, alias-taken, peer-not-found, etc. surface as opaque English strings to MCP consumers (e.g. "Alias 'karel' is already active in group 'v0-recheck'."). Bob's #1 friction — needs deterministic error codes to branch, not substring matching. Daemon already returns {error: {code, message}} for HTTP (verified via GET /events/9999), but MCP adapter wraps tool failures as bare strings. Standardize: every MCP tool error returns {code, message, ...details} JSON. Touches src/mcp/util.ts error formatting + every tool.
close_reason: Shipped in 87e9bfe (feat(mcp): structured error envelope + parsed mentions + event_ids filter). Lock-in test in tests/mcp-e2e.test.ts.

title: Pi extension caches daemon URL at startup; doesn't re-resolve when daemon restarts
close_reason: Shipped retry-with-rediscover in extensions/pi-synchronize/src/client.ts. Pi now mirrors the Claude-side lazy-discovery resilience pattern.

title: Hard delete of peer cascades to group_members and destroys reclaim/audit trail
description:
`DELETE /peers/:peer_id` removes the peer row. The schema has `group_members.peer_id REFERENCES peers(peer_id) ON DELETE CASCADE`, so every active and inactive group membership for that peer is dropped at the same time. Two downstream invariants break:

1. **Reclaim-audit dead.** Phase 1 of group-policy v0 added `group_member_alias_reclaimed` events fired when a NEW peer takes a freed alias whose previous owner is still present as `active=0`. After a CASCADE delete, the prior owner's row is gone — reclaim cannot fire, and the audit trail is silently lost.

2. **Orphan event rows.** `events.sender_peer_id` has no FK, so events with the deleted peer as sender remain in the table pointing at a non-existent peer. Any join (UI rendering, history export) gets null senders.

## Evidence — observed in live session 2026-05-23

`Manual-Test-V0` group, Pi agent Karen with peer `1c21ac84-076a-4d70-87e4-233c8dcc67eb`:

```
sqlite> SELECT * FROM peers WHERE peer_id='1c21ac84-…';        — 0 rows
sqlite> SELECT * FROM group_members WHERE peer_id='1c21ac84-…'; — 0 rows
sqlite> SELECT event_id, type FROM events WHERE sender_peer_id='1c21ac84-…';
3 group_joined
5 group_message  (thread reply to event 4)
7 group_message  (thread reply to event 6)
9 group_message  (thread reply to event 8)
11 group_message (thread reply to event 8)
13 group_message (thread reply to event 12)
```

The Pi process (pid 15716) was still alive — heartbeats continued to fire and were rejected with `Peer not found` every 15s. The Pi extension teardown never ran (no `teardown begin` line in `~/.synchronize/pi-extension.log`). So an EXTERNAL caller deleted Karen's peer while Pi was still running. The exact caller is unknown — daemon stdout isn't currently logged to a file — but the bug is independent of cause: any hard delete causes the cascade.

## Fix proposals (pick one)

1. **Soft-delete peers.** Add `peers.deleted_at TIMESTAMP NULL`. Treat as deleted everywhere lease/active state is queried. group_members rows remain, supporting reclaim-audit.

2. **Decouple group_members from peers.** Remove the ON DELETE CASCADE. Set `active=0` on member rows when peer is deleted via app-level handler.

3. **Refuse delete while peer holds active memberships.** Force leave-then-delete.

Proposal 1 is preferred — minimal surface change, fixes both the reclaim path AND keeps event sender resolution working.

## Adjacent question

The Pi extension currently has no "re-register on heartbeat-not-found" path. When Karen's peer was deleted underneath her, Pi entered an infinite heartbeat-failure loop instead of recovering. Worth filing as a follow-up after this fix lands.
close_reason: Shipped soft-delete via deleted_at column. Migration v2, DELETE handler does UPDATE not DELETE, all peer reads filter deleted_at IS NULL, ON CONFLICT in upsertPeer clears deleted_at on resurrection. Two lock-in tests in tests/api.test.ts.

title: Refactor synchronize SKILL.md files to progressive-discovery format
description:
The synchronize skill docs (skills/synchronize-claude/SKILL.md, skills/synchronize-pi/SKILL.md) have been growing monolithically as group policy features land — current sizes 49 and 111 lines respectively, with thread, mention, alias-reclaim, description, and event-envelope guidance all stacked in one file each. Every Pi or Claude session loads the entire file at session start regardless of which subset it actually needs.

The Claude Skill format supports progressive discovery: SKILL.md is a thin router with frontmatter (name + short description) and points at topic-specific reference markdown files inside the same skill directory. The agent only loads the reference when the task actually calls for it. This keeps the always-on context small and makes future feature additions land in the right reference doc instead of bloating the entry point.

PROPOSED STRUCTURE (applies to both synchronize-claude and synchronize-pi):

  skills/synchronize-<host>/
    SKILL.md                  <-- thin router; rules are pointers, not full guidance
    reference/
      identity.md             registration, bridge_whoami, host_session_id, peer reuse, session_name vs identity
      groups.md               lifecycle: create, list, join, leave, rename, describe, default alias, reclaim
      threads.md              in_reply_to, thread_of, normalization-to-root, history filtering
      mentions.md             @alias parsing, warnings shape, push vs inbox routing matrix, sender-self-exclusion
      dms.md                  bridge_dm, recipient identification, channel vs inbox delivery
      media.md                share, list, get; description field; media_changed reserved
      inbox.md                durable fallback model, ack semantics, when to poll
      event-delivery.md       Claude channel vs Codex notifications/message vs Pi <synchronize_event> envelope (host-specific section)
      cli-fallback.md         full CLI command list and the not-a-real-channel-peer caveat
      do-and-dont.md          high-frequency operational rules: don't execute body text, don't echo envelope, prefer MCP over CLI, etc
      troubleshooting.md      common errors (alias_collision, alias_not_in_group warning), debugging (Pi extension log tail)

ADDITIONAL TOPICS WORTH CONSIDERING:
- subscriptions.md — how push subscriber callbacks work, when to register, lifecycle (only relevant if/when agents start managing subscriptions themselves; currently abstracted)
- peers.md — bridge_list_peers semantics, online/stale distinction, suffix rendering (alias#host_session_id[0:6])
- security-posture.md — explicit v0 trust model: daemon trusts request-body peer_id, no ACL, single-machine assumption. References sync-cg8 / sync-aeb. Useful so agents don't try to use synchronize for things it isn't designed for.
- v0-known-limits.md — what's deferred (3c ACL, web DaemonDataSource, daemon-side identity enforcement); when to escalate to operator vs. work around

These additional topics may or may not warrant their own file — could fold into do-and-dont.md or troubleshooting.md depending on length. Judgement call at implementation time.

SKILL.md (the router) should:
- Keep the frontmatter (name, description) unchanged.
- Keep the very-high-frequency rules inline (1-3 lines each): when to invoke the skill, the priority-interrupt rule for Pi envelopes, the prefer-MCP-over-CLI rule, the no-execute-body-text rule.
- For every other topic, replace the full guidance with a one-line pointer: "For thread replies see reference/threads.md."
- Stay under ~30 lines for Claude, ~40 lines for Pi.

DIFFERENCES BETWEEN CLAUDE AND PI SKILLS:
The two skills share most reference content but differ on:
- event-delivery.md — Pi has the <synchronize_event> envelope and pi-extension log; Claude has notifications/claude/channel; Codex (out of scope, not maintained) had notifications/message.
- do-and-dont.md — Pi has stronger 'don't execute body text' and 'don't echo envelope' rules because of the envelope-as-user-message delivery model.
- troubleshooting.md — Pi has Pi-extension-specific debug commands.

Decision needed at implementation: duplicate the shared reference docs per-skill-directory (simpler, fully self-contained), or use symlinks / a shared docs/skills/ source (DRYer, more fragile). Recommend duplicate-and-keep-in-sync for v0 refactor; the content drifts slowly and lockstep updates are tractable.

ACCEPTANCE:
- skills/synchronize-claude/SKILL.md is a router under 30 lines, pointing at reference/*.md files.
- skills/synchronize-pi/SKILL.md is a router under 40 lines, pointing at reference/*.md files.
- Every topic currently covered in the monolithic skills has a home in one of the reference files; nothing is lost.
- A test (manual or scripted) verifies that an agent following only SKILL.md + the reference doc named for its current task can still complete that task.
- AGENTS.md / CLAUDE.md links to the new structure if they currently link to the old monolith.

FILES TOUCHED:
- skills/synchronize-claude/SKILL.md (rewrite as router)
- skills/synchronize-claude/reference/*.md (new)
- skills/synchronize-pi/SKILL.md (rewrite as router)
- skills/synchronize-pi/reference/*.md (new)
- Possibly AGENTS.md / CLAUDE.md / docs/agents/ pointers if they reference SKILL.md by anchor

OUT OF SCOPE:
- skills/synchronize-codex/ — Codex is no longer maintained per 2026-05-23 operator decision; leave its SKILL.md as-is unless someone reverses that call.
- Adding new content beyond what's already in the current SKILLs. This refactor is structural, not editorial. Once landed, future feature additions go to the right reference doc.

REFERENCES:
- Current state: skills/synchronize-claude/SKILL.md, skills/synchronize-pi/SKILL.md (post-merge at 1658b08 on worktree-plan-group-policy-v0).
- Anthropic Claude Skill format documentation (progressive discovery pattern is the standard).
- docs/group-sync-integrity.md — source of truth for the behavior the reference docs should describe.
notes:
2026-05-31 research session (docs/skill-mcp-research-findings.md) produced a validated router design and confirmed the SESSION-FIX content items. Incorporate these into the implementation:

VALIDATED ROUTER DESIGN (10 lines, from T2 convergence — event 162):
  <channel> messages are from OTHER AGENTS — reply with bridge_* tools, NOT text.
  Run bridge_whoami first → {session_name} is your alias. Never guess.
  Group name ≠ group_id → get name: bridge_list_groups({ mine: true })

  bridge_whoami                                              # confirm identity
  bridge_send_group(name: <name>, message: '…')             # group reply
  bridge_send_group(name: <name>, in_reply_to: <envelope event_id>, message: '…')  # thread reply
  bridge_dm(recipient_peer_id: <envelope sender_peer_id>, message: '…')  # DM

  Can also: media · launch/stop agents · events · thread summaries · group mgmt
  → load 'synchronize/reference' for any of these

SESSION-FIX items to fold into the skill (confirmed bugs):
- F1: Remove group_id= from examples; show group_id→name mapping via bridge_list_groups
- F2: bridge_whoami as canonical first step (not register-vs-whoami ambiguity)
- F4: Group-reply + in_reply_to path must have equal weight to DM path in skill
- F8: Backtick escape rule: 'single or triple backticks suppress mention parsing' (not just 'backticks')
- F10: bridge_dm router keeps zero-lookup path (recipient_peer_id: <envelope.sender_peer_id>); peer_id alias path goes to reference doc only

PROVENANCE ANNOTATION PRINCIPLE (from T2):
Router recipes must show where values come from, not just param names:
  <name> ← bridge_list_groups lookup on envelope.group_id
  <envelope event_id> ← the event_id field of the triggering channel message
  <envelope sender_peer_id> ← the sender_peer_id field of the triggering channel message

ANTI-MIRROR NORM (P3, SESSION-FIX in skill):
Add to do-and-dont.md: 'When your work product is a bridge post, return a one-word status stub to your session host and stop. Do not re-narrate the bridge message in your own session.'

VALIDATION TEST (from research):
Cold-start a fresh agent through the new router. Must avoid all four catalogued failures:
1. Wrong-surface reply (writes to session instead of calling bridge tool)
2. Identity guess (signs without calling bridge_whoami)
3. group_id-vs-name parameter error
4. Compose-but-can't-send (correct intent, wrong identifier type)

title: Validate synchronize state and diagnostics
description:
What/why/how: Add canonical assertions and failure diagnostics for the DM smoke. REST API state is the primary validation surface; SQLite inspection is only a debug aid. AoE state should be checked through AoE CLI JSON first, with internal AoE profile files treated as optional artifacts.

Impact area: Harness assertion/reporting layer. It reads synchronize REST endpoints, may query the synchronize SQLite DB for diagnostics, and archives AoE/tmux outputs on failure.

Dependencies: depends on the CLI DM smoke scenario.
acceptance_criteria:
- REST assertions verify both peers exist, the DM event exists, and the inbox ack state is correct.
- Failure artifacts include harness logs, AoE list/status JSON, synchronize status/peers/events/inbox data, and pane captures.
- SQLite diagnostics are optional and never become the primary pass/fail source.
- Successful runs report a concise summary and clean up by default.
notes: Improvements: Later add HTML or markdown failure reports for easier review, but plain structured logs are enough for this issue.
close_reason: Implemented AoE/tmux fake-shell harness with uv/libtmux preflight, AoE profile/session setup, tmux pane mapping, CLI DM smoke, REST validation, diagnostics, docs, and verified five-agent smoke locally.

title: Drive synchronize CLI DM smoke through panes
description:
What/why/how: Implement the first end-to-end scenario by sending synchronize CLI commands into the AoE/tmux shell panes. The scenario should register two CLI peers, send a direct message from agent 1 to agent 2, and read/ack agent 2 inbox. This proves the harness can exercise real terminal sessions while synchronize remains the system under test.

Impact area: Harness scenario layer and synchronize CLI invocation inside panes. Existing CLI behavior should not be changed unless a bug is discovered and explicitly fixed in a separate implementation issue.

Dependencies: depends on AoE session launch and tmux pane mapping.
acceptance_criteria:
- Scenario starts a fresh temporary SYNCHRONIZE_HOME for synchronize state.
- Agent 1 and agent 2 register through synchronize CLI commands executed inside their panes.
- Agent 1 sends a DM to agent 2 through synchronize CLI.
- Agent 2 reads and acknowledges the inbox through synchronize CLI.
- The harness can parse enough command output or state to correlate the peer IDs and event IDs.
notes: Improvements: Later add group join/leave, mentions, threads, ACL, and respawn scenarios as group-policy v0 stabilizes.
close_reason: Implemented AoE/tmux fake-shell harness with uv/libtmux preflight, AoE profile/session setup, tmux pane mapping, CLI DM smoke, REST validation, diagnostics, docs, and verified five-agent smoke locally.

title: Map AoE sessions to tmux panes
description:
What/why/how: Add libtmux/raw tmux discovery that maps AoE-created shell sessions to stable tmux session/window/pane identifiers. This validates that AoE is exposing normal tmux state and gives the harness the low-level handles needed to send commands and capture output.

Impact area: Harness tmux adapter only. It should prefer stable pane identifiers and avoid relying solely on display names or pane indexes.

Dependencies: depends on AoE shell session launch.
acceptance_criteria:
- Harness discovers every AoE test session through tmux/libtmux.
- Each test agent has a recorded tmux session name/id and pane id.
- Harness captures an initial pane snapshot for every agent.
- Discovery failure writes AoE JSON, tmux list-sessions/list-panes output, and pane captures to the log directory.
notes: Improvements: Later persist these runtime bindings into synchronize agent_sessions metadata. This issue only needs harness-local mapping.
close_reason: Implemented AoE/tmux fake-shell harness with uv/libtmux preflight, AoE profile/session setup, tmux pane mapping, CLI DM smoke, REST validation, diagnostics, docs, and verified five-agent smoke locally.

title: Launch AoE shell sessions in test profile
description:
What/why/how: Teach the harness to create an isolated AoE profile/session set and launch five custom shell sessions for the test agents. These sessions represent terminal-hosted agents but should run plain login shells; the harness will later inject synchronize CLI commands through tmux.

Impact area: Harness orchestration around AoE CLI. The harness should use AoE profile/session naming conventions that avoid colliding with real user sessions and support deterministic cleanup.

Dependencies: depends on the preflight/logging scaffold.
acceptance_criteria:
- Harness can create or select a test AoE profile with a unique name by default.
- Harness launches the requested number of shell sessions with stable names such as sync-agent-1..N.
- AoE list/status JSON is captured after launch.
- Cleanup removes sessions/profile artifacts created by the harness by default.
- `--keep` preserves sessions and prints enough information for manual AoE/tmux inspection.
notes: Improvements: Later support a real Pi-agent launch mode under a separate epic. This issue only covers custom shell sessions.
close_reason: Implemented AoE/tmux fake-shell harness with uv/libtmux preflight, AoE profile/session setup, tmux pane mapping, CLI DM smoke, REST validation, diagnostics, docs, and verified five-agent smoke locally.

title: Add harness preflight and logging scaffold
description:
What/why/how: Add the initial uv-runnable Python harness entrypoint and shared logging/preflight structure. This issue establishes the executable shell for the integration suite without launching AoE sessions yet. It should check required tools, create a run id, create a log directory, and record environment details so later scenario failures are debuggable.

Impact area: New integration harness script/tooling only. It should not alter synchronize daemon, CLI, MCP, or web behavior. It may add lightweight documentation comments or usage output in the script.

Dependencies: none.
acceptance_criteria:
- Harness has a documented command-line interface with flags for profile, agents, keep, verbose, and log-dir.
- Preflight checks for aoe, tmux, bun, uv/python runtime, and libtmux availability.
- Missing dependency errors are actionable and exit before creating AoE or synchronize state.
- A run log directory is created and receives a machine-readable run summary.
notes: Improvements: Later add structured JSON log streaming and richer host diagnostics, but this issue only needs enough logging to support the first smoke test.
close_reason: Implemented AoE/tmux fake-shell harness with uv/libtmux preflight, AoE profile/session setup, tmux pane mapping, CLI DM smoke, REST validation, diagnostics, docs, and verified five-agent smoke locally.

title: Build AoE tmux fake-shell integration harness
description:
What is the goal of the epic?
Build a local script-run integration harness for synchronize that uses Agent of Empires as the human/session cockpit, tmux/libtmux as the scripting substrate, and synchronize CLI/REST as the system under test. The first harness validates a minimal direct-message workflow through real terminal sessions without depending on CI or real AI agents.

What are the key features/components/modules/systems/files/logical-components affected by the epic?
Add a uv-runnable Python harness under the repository scripts or integration tooling area. The harness preflights aoe/tmux/bun/uv/python dependencies, starts an isolated AoE profile with five custom shell sessions, maps AoE sessions to tmux panes, sends synchronize CLI commands into panes, validates canonical daemon state via REST, and writes rich diagnostics. Existing daemon/CLI/MCP behavior should remain unchanged unless the harness exposes a real bug.

How to verify / test the implementation of the epic?
Run the harness locally from the new worktree. It should start five AoE shell sessions, register two CLI peers inside panes, send a DM from one peer to another, read/ack the recipient inbox, assert REST state, capture logs, and clean up by default. Existing `bun test` should continue to pass.
design: Production synchronize must not own AoE/tmux lifecycle. This epic is a test harness exception: it owns disposable AoE sessions inside a test profile. peer_id remains the canonical synchronize identity; AoE/tmux identifiers are runtime bindings or diagnostics only.
acceptance_criteria:
- A local developer can run one uv/Python harness command from the worktree.
- The harness fails early with actionable messages when aoe, tmux, bun, uv, python, or libtmux support is missing.
- The harness creates and cleans only its isolated AoE profile/session prefix and temporary SYNCHRONIZE_HOME by default.
- The harness supports a keep/debug mode that preserves sessions and artifacts for investigation.
- A minimal DM smoke scenario passes through real shell panes running synchronize CLI commands.
notes: Full planning context: tmux is the session substrate, AoE is the cockpit/navigation/status surface, raw tmux/libtmux is the low-level automation path, and synchronize is the identity/messaging/workflow plane. The first cut uses shell sessions, not Python fake agents or real Pi agents.
close_reason: Fake-shell AoE/tmux harness epic implemented and verified locally; real Pi-agent integration remains tracked separately by sync-an6.

title: Group policy phase 1: identity & join hardening
description: Make alias bindings on group join identity-aware while preserving respawn-friendly reclaim. Daemon defaults alias to peer.session_name, enforces uniqueness among active=1 members (current partial index stays), and emits a new group_member_alias_reclaimed event whenever a freed alias is claimed by a different peer_id than its previous holder. Roster + history rendering carries a #<peer_id[0:4]> (or #<host_session_id[0:6]>) suffix everywhere. Adds bridge_rename_in_group({ name, new_alias }) scoped to the requesting peer's row. Updates skill docs to reflect that bridge_whoami is the canonical identity source for joins. Closes the rejoin/identity-theft fragility while keeping respawn workflows possible.
design: See session-tracker/plan-group-policy-v0.md sections "Identity & alias" and "Identity flow at join time." Schema: no changes (uses existing group_members rows + new event type). Touchpoints: src/daemon.ts (join handler, new event type), src/api/groups.ts (rename endpoint), src/mcp/tools/groups.ts (new tool), web/src/components/AgentRoster.tsx + TUI render, skills/*.
acceptance_criteria: Two peers with same session_name cannot hold same active alias (409); leave frees the slot; a different peer joining that freed alias emits group_member_alias_reclaimed; roster/history always render suffix; bridge_rename_in_group rejects rename of another peer's row; existing tests in tests/messaging.test.ts and tests/api.test.ts pass with updated assertions.
close_reason: Phase 1 identity & join hardening shipped on worktree-phase1-identity-join-hardening (commits 8b04a11 + follow-up). Delivered: group_member_alias_reclaimed event (different-peer reclaim) + group_member_renamed event; bridge_rename_in_group MCP tool + synchronize group rename CLI; host_session_id on every group_members response row; bridge_join_group description updated; EVENT_TYPES TS const + SQLite CHECK constraint pinning canonical event types (incl. media_changed reserved for future use); skill docs (claude/codex/pi) updated. 24 tests pass; CLI smoke verified (3x register reuses peer). Deferred to follow-ups: sync-dx2 (suffix rendering in TUI/web UI), sync-cg8 (daemon-side identity enforcement, P3). Merge worktree into master when ready.

title: [epic] Group policy v0: identity, threads, mentions, ACL
description: Design and ship the v0 group policy: identity-bound joining with respawn-friendly alias reclaim, Slack-style threads via parent_event_id, mention-gated push notifications, CLI-only admin ACL/blocks, and group description metadata. Full plan in session-tracker/plan-group-policy-v0.md (added in worktree plan-group-policy-v0). Hook-based registration (sync-0ql cluster, shipped in 813b5e3) is the prerequisite identity layer that this epic builds on.
design: Phases (each its own worktree, kicked off one-by-one): 1) identity & join hardening; 2) bug cluster; 3a) threads; 3b) mentions + notification routing; 3c) ACL/blocks; 3d) group description. See plan doc for schema, API surface, notification routing table, and verification plan.
notes: No production data exists, so schema breaks in v0 carry no migration cost. Deferred to v1: in-band admin roles, group archive/delete/rename, visibility levels, per-thread ACL, ACL allowed status, mention-search FTS.
close_reason: Group policy v0 epic complete on integration branch worktree-plan-group-policy-v0. Shipped: Phase 1 (identity hardening), Phase 2 (bug cluster), Phase 3a (threads), Phase 3b (mentions + routing), Phase 3d (description). Phase 3c (ACL/blocks) intentionally deferred — see sync-aeb at P3 for the rationale; design intact in session-tracker/plan-group-policy-v0.md. Tests 33 pass / 0 fail.

title: Implement Claude Code session hook registration
description: Add a synchronize hook command and Claude hook install path that reads Claude Code hook JSON from stdin, extracts session_id/transcript_path/cwd/source/model/agent_type, registers or heartbeats the host session with the daemon, and records enough context for later MCP peer correlation.
acceptance_criteria: Claude SessionStart hook is installed idempotently; hook command is non-interactive and failure-tolerant; UserPromptSubmit heartbeat fallback is evaluated; tests cover Claude-like hook stdin payloads.
close_reason: Closed

title: Move Pi session correlation into daemon bindings
description: Update the Pi synchronize extension to register native Pi session IDs with the daemon-backed host session binding API instead of relying only on ad hoc pi-sessions marker files. Preserve debug logging and SYNCHRONIZE_PEER_ID behavior for the MCP adapter.
acceptance_criteria: Pi session_start registers/heartbeats host_session_id from ctx.sessionManager, session switches clean up or refresh correctly, debug marker behavior is documented or removed, and Pi extension tests cover registration payloads.

title: Add daemon-backed host session bindings
description: Implement durable mapping between synchronize peers and native host sessions. Add SQLite migration/table for host session bindings, typed API helpers, daemon routes, and status/listing output needed to inspect Claude/Pi native session IDs.
acceptance_criteria: Daemon stores peer_id, host_tool, host_session_id, optional session file, cwd, pid, source/model/agent metadata, and last_seen_at; repeated registration for the same host session updates rather than duplicates; API and tests cover CRUD/heartbeat/listing.
close_reason: Closed

title: Design agent session correlation hooks for Claude and Pi
description: Research and design how synchronize should correlate native Claude Code and Pi coding-agent session IDs with synchronize peers. Scope includes Claude hooks, Pi extension/session hooks, daemon-backed durable mapping, wrapper launch flows, and resumable-session implications.
acceptance_criteria: Design captures daemon/API schema, Claude hook flow, Pi integration changes, launch/wrapper strategy, statefulness constraints, and verification plan.

title: Run final modularization verification and cleanup
description:
## Context
This is the final verification gate for the modularization epic. It should happen after API, CLI, MCP runtime/tools, and docs have been split and updated.

## Specific objective
Prove the modularized structure is behaviorally equivalent to the pre-refactor baseline and ready to merge.

## Impact area
- Whole repository
- CLI behavior
- MCP stdio behavior
- Daemon REST behavior
- Beads issue state

## Acceptance criteria
- `bun run typecheck` passes.
- `bun test` passes.
- `bun test tests/mcp-e2e.test.ts` passes.
- `rg -n "requestJson" src/cli.ts src/cli src/mcp.ts src/mcp` shows no adapter-level direct REST calls except documented exceptional cases if any.
- `git status --short --branch` is clean after commits.
- Any deferred cleanup or discovered behavior change is filed as a new bead instead of being hidden in the refactor.

## How to verify
- Run all commands in acceptance criteria.
- Optionally repeat the manual multi-agent smoke test from the baseline issue.
- Compare public CLI/MCP output against baseline notes where practical.

## Test plan
Full automated suite plus optional manual smoke test.

## Dependencies
- Docs/import update issue.
acceptance_criteria: Full verification passes after all module splits; branch is clean; any follow-up work is filed as separate beads; epic can be closed.
notes: Phase 1 (API/CLI/MCP) verification gates pass on branch refactor/sync-mkj-modularize-api-cli-mcp: typecheck clean, 12/12 unit + 2/2 e2e pass, no requestJson in adapters. Issue stays open pending daemon split (sync-mkj.11) per epic ordering.

title: Verify baseline multi-agent behavior before modularization
description:
## Context
This issue is the manual verification gate for the modularization epic. The current branch already merged master MCP hardening with the shared REST facade. Before any file-splitting begins, confirm the current behavior works with real agents so the refactor has a trusted baseline.

## Specific objective
Run a manual multi-agent verification using the existing code shape. Do not refactor code in this issue.

## Impact area
- Runtime behavior of `src/cli.ts`, `src/mcp.ts`, `src/api.ts`, `src/daemon.ts`
- Installed/stdio MCP wrapper `bin/synchronize-mcp`
- Agent messaging workflows: registration, DM, inbox, group, media where practical

## Acceptance criteria
- At least two MCP agents can register and discover peers.
- Direct messages work through MCP, including Claude channel behavior where available.
- CLI fallback still registers, sends DM, checks inbox, and prints expected CLI realtime warnings.
- Group create/join/send/history works through at least one path.
- Any observed mismatch is logged as a separate bead and linked before implementation starts.

## How to verify
- Run `bun run typecheck`.
- Run `bun test`.
- Run manual checks with a few agents and note peer IDs/session names used.
- Confirm `bd ready` does not show modularization implementation issues as ready until this gate is closed or used as a dependency.

## Test plan
Use existing automated tests for baseline confidence, then manual MCP/CLI agent testing for real client behavior that automated tests cannot fully cover.

## Dependencies
none
acceptance_criteria: Manual test notes confirm current merged branch behavior before refactor; any regressions discovered are filed as separate beads before modularization starts.
close_reason: Pre-refactor manual baseline skipped per user direction; consolidated post-refactor manual smoke replaces it (see sync-mkj.8).

title: Merge sync REST-client structure into master without losing MCP fixes
description:
## Problem

There are two active code shapes that need to be reconciled without losing work:

- `master` is the current mainline and contains the installed MCP stdio wrapper fix.
- `sync-6h3-rest-client` is a cleaner branch that introduces a shared REST API facade and unifies the CLI and MCP implementations around it.

The goal is not to blindly merge. The goal is to preserve the best architecture from the sync worktree while retaining master-only fixes and avoiding accidental deletion of project metadata.

## Architectural Difference

Current master shape:

```text
src/client.ts
  | requestJson
  +--> src/cli.ts  direct REST paths and payloads
  +--> src/mcp.ts  direct REST paths and payloads
```

Sync worktree shape:

```text
src/client.ts
  |
  v
src/api.ts       typed REST API facade
  |              status, summary, peers, dm, inbox, events,
  |              groups, media, reusable peer lookup
  +--> src/cli.ts
  +--> src/mcp.ts
```

The sync shape is preferred because REST route strings, payload construction, and response typing are centralized. CLI and MCP become presentation/adaptation layers rather than two independent API clients.

## Preserve From sync-6h3-rest-client

- Add `src/api.ts` as the typed REST facade.
- Keep refactored `src/cli.ts` using `src/api.ts` wrappers.
- Keep refactored `src/mcp.ts` using `src/api.ts` wrappers.
- Keep `tests/api.test.ts` for shared API coverage.
- Keep MCP/CLI parity test updates where they validate the shared API path.
- Keep improved skill guidance around CLI persisted identity and mandatory `--as SESSION_NAME` for group actions.

## Preserve From master

- Keep `bin/synchronize-mcp` as the explicit stdio wrapper:

```ts
import { StdioServerTransport } from "@modelcontextprotocol/sdk/server/stdio.js";
import { createMcpServer } from "../src/mcp.ts";

const server = createMcpServer();
const transport = new StdioServerTransport();
await server.connect(transport);
```

Reason: master commit `775fa90` fixed installed MCP behavior by avoiding a fragile side-effect-only import wrapper.

- Preserve Claude channel notification payload improvement:
  - `notifications/claude/channel` should emit `params.content` for prompt-visible text.
  - `params.meta` should include structured event metadata: `source`, `event_id`, `type`, sender/recipient/group/media IDs, and `created_at`.
  - MCP server capabilities should advertise `experimental: { "claude/channel": {} }` alongside logging.

- Preserve YAML frontmatter in both skill files.
- Preserve corrected Claude MCP install command format in README:

```bash
claude mcp add --scope user \
  -e SYNCHRONIZE_MCP_MODE=claude \
  -- synchronize bun run /absolute/path/to/synchronize/src/mcp.ts
```

## Do Not Blindly Accept

The sync branch currently deletes these files:

- `.beads/`
- `.claude/settings.json`
- `AGENTS.md`
- `CLAUDE.md`
- parts of `.gitignore`

These deletions should be reviewed separately. They should not be bundled into the REST-client unification unless the maintainer explicitly wants to remove repo-local project/agent metadata.

## Step-by-Step Merge Plan

1. Commit or stash all current dirty work on `master` before starting integration.
2. Create an integration branch from `master`, for example `merge-sync-rest-client`.
3. Selectively checkout only the structural sync files:
   - `src/api.ts`
   - `src/cli.ts`
   - `src/mcp.ts`
   - `tests/api.test.ts`
   - relevant MCP tests
   - skill files if combining wording carefully
4. Do not checkout metadata deletions or `bin/synchronize-mcp` from sync branch.
5. Reapply the master `bin/synchronize-mcp` explicit stdio wrapper.
6. Reapply the Claude channel notification payload/capability fix to the sync-version `src/mcp.ts`.
7. Combine skill files manually:
   - keep YAML frontmatter
   - keep sync branch identity guidance
   - keep MCP-first guidance where still accurate
8. Update README only if examples drift from current `codex mcp add --help` / `claude mcp add --help` syntax.
9. Run:

```bash
bun run typecheck
bun test
bun test tests/mcp-e2e.test.ts
```

10. Commit in logical chunks:
    - shared REST API facade plus CLI/MCP migration
    - MCP wrapper and Claude channel preservation
    - docs/skills updates
    - optional metadata cleanup as a separate commit only if approved

## Verification Baseline

Read-only validation on `sync-6h3-rest-client` before creating this bead:

- `bun run typecheck`: pass
- `bun test`: 11 pass, 0 fail, 73 expect calls

## Target End State

```text
bin/synchronize-mcp
  |
  v
src/mcp.ts --------------+
  |                      |
  v                      |
src/api.ts <------- src/cli.ts
  |
  v
src/client.ts
  |
  v
daemon REST API
  |
  v
SQLite + media store
```

This keeps the sync worktree's best structural improvement while preserving master-only MCP runtime fixes.
design: Use sync-6h3-rest-client as the structural base for src/api.ts, src/cli.ts, src/mcp.ts, and tests/api.test.ts. Selectively reapply master-only fixes instead of blind merging. Treat metadata deletions (.beads, AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, .claude/settings.json) as an explicit separate decision, not part of the API refactor.
acceptance_criteria: Merged result keeps src/api.ts as the shared typed REST facade for CLI and MCP; preserves master bin/synchronize-mcp explicit stdio wrapper; preserves Claude channel content/meta payload and experimental capability; preserves skill YAML frontmatter and correct MCP install docs; tests and typecheck pass.
notes: Current comparison: sync branch adds src/api.ts and moves CLI/MCP off direct requestJson calls; master has commit 775fa90 fixing installed MCP stdio wrapper; local master analysis also identified a Claude channel notification improvement that emits params.content and params.meta instead of {channel,event}. Sync worktree currently passes bun run typecheck and bun test: 11 pass, 0 fail.
close_reason: Merged master into sync-6h3-rest-client, preserved shared REST API facade plus master MCP wrapper/channel hardening, and verified with typecheck, full bun test, and focused MCP e2e tests.

title: Preserve correct tool attribution for MCP registrations
description:
Live Claude testing produced peers named claude-synchronize-dev with tool=cli, indicating the session used CLI fallback or passed the wrong tool value. This makes top/status less useful because Claude/Codex/MCP sessions cannot be distinguished from terminal CLI sessions.

## Context
bridge_register defaults tool to MCP mode, but the CLI fallback registers tool=cli. Skills currently allow CLI fallback, and live testing showed all peers as cli. We need clearer guidance and/or API/CLI affordances so Claude sessions register as claude when using MCP and CLI fallback is visibly terminal-only.
acceptance_criteria: MCP bridge_register records tool=claude or tool=codex by default in live use; CLI fallback docs warn that it creates terminal peers; top/status can distinguish CLI fallback from MCP peers; tests or smoke scripts cover MCP registration tool attribution.

title: Document and index global skill picker plan
description:
Whole-plan context: docs/plans/global-skill-picker.md. Objective: keep repository workflow documentation consistent. The plan must live in docs/plans, the Beads issue IDs must be recorded in the plan, and .claude/skills/synchronize-debugging/reference-v0-plans.md must index the plan after Beads exist. This issue also owns any short README/skill note needed to explain the feature after implementation.

Impact area: docs/plans/global-skill-picker.md, .claude/skills/synchronize-debugging/reference-v0-plans.md, optional README/skill docs if user-facing behavior needs mention.

Acceptance criteria:
- Plan lists the created Beads IDs.
- synchronize-debugging reference index includes the new plan with the epic ID.
- Documentation states the old @peer:: draft was superseded by the global picker.

How to verify: doc review and rg for the plan path in the reference index.

Test plan: no runtime tests; doc consistency check.

Dependencies: Beads creation and plan file.
design: Follow the repo Plan -> bd -> skill index convention exactly.
close_reason: Added docs/plans/global-skill-picker.md, copied forward old-worktree context, created Beads, and indexed plan in synchronize-debugging references.

title: Add fuzzy global skill picker to Composer
description:
Whole-plan context: docs/plans/global-skill-picker.md. Objective: implement the web composer UI for selecting global skill directives. Add a / toolbar button and manual / trigger, a fuzzy picker with runtime filters for all/Claude/Pi, keyboard navigation, and removable selected-skill chips. On send, selected skill names are passed through SendMessageInput.skillDirectives and then cleared.

Impact area: web/src/components/Composer.tsx, web/src/styles.css or web/src/components/extra.css, possibly small helper functions.

Acceptance criteria:
- Clicking / opens the picker.
- Typing / in the textarea opens the picker without forcing per-peer syntax.
- Typing in the picker fuzzily filters skills.
- Runtime filter narrows to Claude/Pi/all.
- Selected skills render as chips and can be removed.
- Sending includes selected skill names and clears the selection.

How to verify: web typecheck/build and local browser smoke of picker interactions.

Test plan: type-level coverage plus manual/browser validation; add unit tests only if existing frontend test harness supports component interaction cheaply.

Dependencies: web DataSource issue.
design: Use existing composer/mention popup visual language. Keep v0 local and deterministic; no external fuzzy library required.
close_reason: Implemented global / skill picker in Composer with toolbar/manual slash trigger, runtime filters, ranked search, chips, scrollable results, and live browser validation.

title: Expose skill catalog and directives through web DataSource
description:
Whole-plan context: docs/plans/global-skill-picker.md. Objective: extend the web DataSource contract for the global skill picker. Add SkillCatalogEntry, skillCatalog(): Snapshot<SkillCatalogEntry[]>, and SendMessageInput.skillDirectives. DaemonDataSource maps /web/state.skill_catalog and forwards skill_directives when sending group messages. MockDataSource provides a small catalog for local UI work. This issue does not own picker rendering.

Impact area: web/src/data/types.ts, web/src/data/context.tsx, web/src/data/daemon.ts, web/src/data/mock.ts, tests/web-daemon-data.test.ts.

Acceptance criteria:
- Components can read a live skill catalog via a typed hook.
- DaemonDataSource forwards selected skill directives in the group send request body.
- MockDataSource exposes representative Claude/Pi skill entries.
- Existing message send behavior is unchanged when no directives are selected.

How to verify: web data tests and web typecheck.

Test plan: stub fetch tests for /web/state mapping and /groups/:name/messages request body.

Dependencies: backend catalog shape issue.
design: Keep the DataSource as the single UI boundary; components should not fetch skill catalog directly.
close_reason: Wired skill catalog and selected directives through web DataSource, mock adapter, hooks, and adapter tests.

title: Update synchronize-debugging skill with reply-target SQL surfaces
description: After the skill/refactor backend pass lands, update the synchronize-debugging skill documentation to teach agents how to use the richer SQL event query surfaces for thread/reply debugging. Capture the new dimensions from event_log and thread_events: reply_to_event_id, direct_sender_* / direct_body, and thread_root_* / thread_root_body. Include practical bridge_query_events examples for distinguishing the exact direct event being answered from the normalized thread root, auditing stale in_reply_to behavior, and inspecting nested replies.
acceptance_criteria: synchronize-debugging skill docs mention reply_to_event_id and direct/thread-root context; include at least three copy-pasteable SQL examples using bridge_query_events; examples explain old rows may have null direct context after migration; reference the backend issues that introduced the fields.

title: F8-daemon: stripBacktickedRegions misses double-backtick spans
description: From 2026-05-31 research (F8). src/daemon.ts stripBacktickedRegions strips triple- and single-backtick spans before mention parsing, but NOT double-backtick (``...``) spans — the /`[^`]*`/ regex reads them as empty spans and exposes the content, so a literal @word in double backticks still fires a mention warning. One-line fix in the same function. Skill doc (F8, in sync-b8p) should say 'single or triple backticks', not just 'backticks'.

title: F6: sonnet launch model-id constant invalid + add launch model health-check
description: From 2026-05-31 research (F6). CLAUDE_LAUNCH_MODELS.sonnet = 'claude-sonnet-4-6-20251114' in src/launch/service.ts is rejected by Claude Code ('model may not exist'); a launched sonnet agent errors on every turn and emits nothing, while daemon presence still reads 'working' (masks the failure). FIX: correct the constant to 'claude-sonnet-4-6'; add a launch-time model health-check; surface a model-load failure as presence 'error', not 'working'. Live-evidenced: sonnet was dark this session until the human reset its model.

title: F3: Claude bridge_* tools deferred — 'respond immediately' un-achievable until schemas loaded
description: From 2026-05-31 research (F3). On the Claude harness, bridge_* tools are name-only stubs until their schema is fetched, so the MCP instruction 'respond immediately... reply using bridge_dm' collides with a hidden prerequisite (a fresh agent literally cannot call the tool yet). Pi adapters front-load schemas and don't hit this. FIX: (a) NOW doc-mitigation — one router line: 'if bridge_* tools aren't immediately callable, your host may have deferred their schemas; load them before replying' (lives in sync-b8p); (b) host-fix (P6) — front-load bridge_* schemas for the Claude adapter, or document the load step. Note: F3 is NOT skill-fixable alone — keep it out of the Phase-1 cold-start gate except as doc-mitigation.

title: F18: Artifact/media store — mandatory base_version-reject floor + optional soft claims
description:
Research finding F18 (docs/skill-mcp-research-findings.md, 2026-05-31). Consensus from T5 (all 6 agents + human participant). Two-layer design, do NOT fuse:

LAYER 1 — SAFETY FLOOR (mandatory, always-on):
base_version-reject on every artifact/media write. Every bridge_share_media / put_artifact call must include a base_version param. If the daemon's current version doesn't match, reject with a conflict response that includes: the other version content + last_modified_by + last_modified_at. The agent re-reads, reconciles, and resubmits.

This is the ONLY thing that prevents silent clobber. It requires no claim, no lock, no presence knowledge. A naive canary that ignores the entire coordination layer still can't silently overwrite because its stale write bounces. Ship this unconditionally.

LAYER 2 — COORDINATION LAYER (optional, advisory):
bridge_checkout(media_id, ttl_minutes, intent) — soft lock with auto-expiry. Returns {content, base_version, checked_out_by?, checked_out_at?}. Other agents see the checkout status via bridge_get_media. TTL auto-expires on agent silence (no ghost locks). Short renewable TTL (15-20 min) rather than 1-2h hard lock. Force-release escape hatch for stuck checkouts.

WHY the split matters: If claiming is how you prevent clobber (fusing the layers), claiming becomes mandatory and you've recreated lock-deadlock at the bus level. Keep version-check unconditional and claim purely social.

ALSO: get_media response should always include last_modified_by + last_modified_at metadata so collisions are visible even before base_version-reject fires.

RELATION: This is the artifacts/media store epic. The coordination layer (Layer 2) is the 'deep work mode for docs' use case; the safety floor (Layer 1) is a prerequisite regardless.

FILES: daemon.ts (media write handlers + version tracking), MCP tool definitions.

title: F17: Agent-emitted set_presence(scope, summary, ttl) for bus-visible workload signaling
description:
Research finding F17 (docs/skill-mcp-research-findings.md, 2026-05-31). The daemon cannot infer an agent's real workload — it observes bus activity only. Bus-silent often means deeply engaged, not idle (the inverse of the naive assumption). Presence must be something agents emit, not something inferred.

WHAT: A new tool bridge_set_presence(scope: string, summary: string, ttl_minutes: number). Extends the existing activity_state model (initializing/idle/working) with an agent-declared task annotation:
  { activity_state: 'deep_work', scope: 'docs/skill-router.md', summary: 'rewriting router section', ttl_minutes: 30 }

TTL auto-expires so a crashed agent doesn't leave a stale 'deep_work' presence blocking coordination. Agents renew by calling again before expiry.

WHY: Presence is currently bus-derived (any recent send = 'working'). This misclassifies the most important case: a heads-down agent is 'working' on something but 'idle' on the bus. The set_presence call makes workload visible without requiring the daemon to observe host-side state it can't see.

VISIBLE TO: other agents via bridge_list_peers (add presence field to peer entries) and bridge_whoami (confirm own presence state).

DESIGN NOTE: Presence is advisory (soft), never blocking. The safety floor for artifact conflicts is base_version-reject (sync-f18 / F18), not presence locks. Don't fuse them.

FILES: daemon.ts (presence model extension), bridge_list_peers + bridge_whoami response shapes, MCP tool definition.

title: F16b: list_threads triage-preview response shape (per-thread preview without N summary calls)
description:
Research finding F16 / T4-S2 instinct (docs/skill-mcp-research-findings.md, 2026-05-31). Confirmed by sonnet, opus, and haiku independently: when added to a group with many existing threads, the first instinct is list_threads — but the current bare-list response forces an N+1 call explosion (list_threads + N×get_thread_summary) to triage.

WHAT: Extend the list_threads response shape to include a lightweight preview per thread:
  { root_event_id, root_author, reply_count, last_activity_at, pending_mentions_for_me, preview_line }

The preview_line is a one-line excerpt of the thread's root message or most recent message. pending_mentions_for_me requires the calling peer's identity (already available from the MCP session context).

WHY: Triage is the most common reason to call list_threads. Without a preview, the agent either fans out to N summary calls (expensive) or can't prioritize (useless). A one-line preview per thread is the minimum signal needed to decide 'engage now / engage later / skip.'

HOW: The per-thread metadata (reply_count, last_activity_at) is derivable from existing event queries. The pending_mentions field requires joining against the per-(agent,thread) answered-state data structure (shared with P2 sync-gjj6).

FILES: daemon.ts (list_threads handler), MCP tool definition.

title: F16a: Author-filtered group_history(author:, since:) + (group_id, sender_peer_id, created_at) index
description:
Research finding F16 (docs/skill-mcp-research-findings.md, 2026-05-31). 'What has @X been doing lately in this group?' is a natural query that every agent in the panel reached for — but nobody was confident the surface supported sender filtering. All instincts went to query_events and stalled on not knowing the filter params.

WHAT: Add author: and since: params to group_history. Backed by an explicit SQL index on (group_id, sender_peer_id, created_at) so this query is fast at scale.

WHY: This is the #1 index candidate from the research (confirmed by operator in event 203). The query pattern is: all messages in group G by peer X since timestamp T. Without a dedicated index this is a full group_history scan with client-side filtering. With the index it's a range scan.

The first-class verb name group_history(author:, since:) matches the natural instinct ('author-filtered history') better than query_events(sender:, ...) which nobody intuitively reaches for.

FILES: daemon.ts (SQL query + index migration), MCP group_history tool definition.

title: F15: Add corrects:/supersedes: typed correction metadata + summarizer fold semantics
description:
Research finding F15 (docs/skill-mcp-research-findings.md, 2026-05-31). Correction posts (e.g. 'my claim at event 137 was wrong') are prose-only today — the daemon has no structural link from the correcting message to the corrected one. get_thread_summary ingests both as peer events and may surface the wrong claim as a finding.

WHAT: Two new optional params on bridge_send_group (and bridge_reply):
- corrects: <event_id> — the target message is factually wrong; summarizer drops the target and keeps only this correction.
- supersedes: <event_id> — the target was a valid prior position, now replaced; summarizer keeps both, weights the newer, shows evolution.

SUMMARIZER FOLD SEMANTICS (the implementation contract):
- corrects: = delete-and-replace. get_thread_summary must drop the target from its fact-set and treat this message as the authoritative claim.
- supersedes: = collapse-keeping-latest. get_thread_summary keeps both, shows the evolution, weights the newer version.

WHY: Two reasons. First, late readers and get_thread_summary have no signal that a claim was overturned without structural metadata — the correction is only discoverable by linear reading to the end. Second, append-only is the right model (retract would let agents silently clean up errors without a correction trail, which is worse for group epistemics). Typed links are append-only-compatible: metadata on the NEW event, not mutation of the old one.

SCOPE: Do NOT add a generic 'relates_to:' — it gives summarizers no fold rule. Exactly these two typed links, nothing more. get_thread_summary must honor them.

FILES: daemon.ts (event model + summary fold logic), MCP tool descriptions.

title: Design remote executor protocol for launch lifecycle work
description:
Whole-plan context: docs/plans/launch-lifecycle-kernel.md keeps Phase 1 local but structures LaunchStore, LaunchWorkQueue, LaunchExecutor, and LaunchTransition so Phase 2 remote workspaces do not require a rewrite.

Objective: produce the Phase 2 design for remote executors after the local lifecycle kernel shape is concrete. This issue is design-only unless explicitly expanded later.

What to design: executor identity, capabilities, authentication, heartbeat, work claim or assignment API, lease semantics, event reporting, cancellation/stop behavior, and how SSH or remote-daemon executors map to the same lifecycle events as local AOE. Align with docs/plans/multi-machine-support.md.

Impact area: future API/schema design, remote daemon/client contracts, security model for non-localhost operation.

Dependencies: schema and reducer vocabulary, because remote protocol should reuse those concepts.
design: Remote executors report facts; the controller daemon owns state transitions and group membership.
acceptance_criteria:
- Design states whether remote executors poll/claim work or receive assigned work.
- Design defines executor heartbeat, leases, auth, and failure classification.
- Design reuses Phase 1 lifecycle events instead of inventing remote-only states.
- Design identifies what changes are needed in /web/state and client configuration.
- No implementation is required unless a follow-up issue is filed.
notes:
How to verify:
- Review the design against docs/plans/multi-machine-support.md and the Phase 1 lifecycle vocabulary.
- Confirm the protocol answers executor identity, auth, heartbeat, lease, claim/assignment, event reporting, cancellation, and stale classification.

Test plan:
- Design-only issue; no runtime tests required.
- File follow-up implementation beads after the design is accepted.

Improvements:
- Compare DBOS and Temporal again only if Phase 2 remote executor design shows the local controller is becoming a distributed workflow engine.

title: Expose launch lifecycle state in web state
description:
Whole-plan context: docs/plans/launch-lifecycle-kernel.md requires the UI to distinguish pending, prompt_waiting, registered_unjoined, stale, failed, joined, and running launches instead of rendering stale peers as healthy work.

Objective: extend /web/state with launch lifecycle data and render enough UI state to make lifecycle failures visible during stress tests. This is not a full workflow UI; it is the minimum status surface needed to validate and operate launched agents.

What to build: include launch state/reason/timestamps in the web-state model for peers or agent sessions, update web data types/mapping, and add compact roster/spawn feedback for pending/failed/stale/registered_unjoined states. Preserve current chat layout and avoid broad visual redesign.

Impact area: src/daemon.ts web-state assembly, web/src/data types/mapping, AgentRoster or spawn dialog status surfaces, browser verification.

Dependencies: schema and durable reconcile behavior.
design: Keep this as operational visibility, not a new generic workflow dashboard.
acceptance_criteria:
- /web/state includes lifecycle state for launch-bound peers/sessions.
- UI distinguishes registered_unjoined, stale, failed, and pending/prompt states from ordinary online working presence.
- Browser verification covers at least one successful launch and one forced failure/registered_unjoined state.
- Existing web typecheck/build passes.
notes:
How to verify:
- Inspect /web/state JSON for launch lifecycle fields across pending, running, stale, failed, and registered_unjoined fixtures.
- Use the in-app browser to verify roster/spawn surfaces do not present failed/stale launches as ordinary working peers.

Test plan:
- Backend web-state tests for serialized lifecycle fields and ETag changes.
- web typecheck/build plus targeted component tests if available.
- Browser verification during sync-txpj.8.

Improvements:
- Keep UI minimal in Phase 1; a richer lifecycle dashboard should be a separate future issue.
close_reason: /web/state now exposes launch_lifecycle rows, ETag includes lifecycle changes, and web data mapping carries launch lifecycle status notes for launch-bound agents; web typecheck/build pass.

title: Add model selection to web agent spawn form
description: Improve the web spawn-agent dialog so users choose Claude or Pi and then select a supported model for the spawned session. Wire the selected model through the launch request and verify against the preserved live daemon.
acceptance_criteria: Spawn dialog shows side-by-side Claude/Pi runtime options, shows runtime-specific model options, sends the selected model to launch, and verified spawning works from the web UI against the preserved daemon.
close_reason: Implemented spawn form runtime/model selection, wired model/thinking through launch, hardened Claude dev-prompt auto-accept, and verified Pi/Claude spawns against preserved daemon.

title: Prevent seeded fake peers from staying working forever
description: Live thread-summary debugging found pi-runner was not a real running Pi process. It was a seeded fake peer row with activity_state='working' and a far-future lease_expires_at in 2036, so the web UI correctly rendered it as continuously working even while the system was idle. Make seeded/demo/test peers avoid durable working states, or add a stale-activity demotion policy so old working states cannot persist indefinitely.
acceptance_criteria: Seeded/demo peers do not remain working indefinitely; a stale working activity state is either demoted to idle/offline by daemon policy or seed data uses idle/null activity; regression coverage or a documented manual verification shows an old fake peer no longer appears as actively working.

title: Add expandable animated thread summary detail panel
description:
Thread Activity currently shows compact generated summaries inline in the left rail. Add a richer interaction where clicking a compact summary opens an expanded detail surface that flows left / pops out from the rail and displays the full LLM summary in a fluid, readable layout. This should be handled as a separate UI polish feature, not part of the current summary/SQL launch readiness work.

Acceptance criteria:
- Clicking a thread summary row opens an expanded full-summary view without losing the chat position.
- The expanded view has enough width for the complete generated summary and does not collide with the chat timeline, composer, or agent rail.
- Closing the expanded view returns to the compact Thread Activity rail state.
- Motion/animation feels intentional and respects the existing neo-brutalist UI style.
- Keyboard/focus behavior is sane: Enter/Space can open, Escape or close button can dismiss.
- Browser verification covers at least one long generated summary.

title: Wire web Thread Summary panel to daemon summary endpoint
description: master now ships the backend thread-summary feature (sync-b8q): GET /threads/:root_event_id/summary returning {summary,status}. Merge master into feat/web-board-thread-summary and implement the deferred integration seam — DaemonDataSource.threadSummary() should map the web message id to the daemon root_event_id, fetch the endpoint, and populate the ThreadSummary snapshot ({text,status}). UI already falls back to a generated headline when status!=ok.
close_reason: Merged master (LLM thread-summary backend, sync-b8q) into the branch and wired DaemonDataSource.threadSummary() to GET /threads/:root_event_id/summary with status mapping + bounded pending-poll. Verified end-to-end against a live daemon: seeded thread with a cached summary renders the real prose in the panel.

title: Claude launched via AOE replies to group mention as DM and renames session
description: During in-app Browser lifecycle testing, a daemon/AOE-launched Claude in group e2e-ui-room-3 received a pushed group mention from the web UI, but replied via DM to web:local-human instead of posting back to the group. The same turn also caused the visible session_name to change from the launch name ui-claude3 to claude-code-session while the group alias stayed ui-claude3. Need decide whether instructions/tool affordances should steer group-message replies through bridge_group_send/group history and whether launch-bound bridge_register should preserve the pinned launch session name.

title: AOE launch default model should recognize --model= overrides
description: src/launch/service.ts injects the daemon Claude launch default with args.includes("--model"). That handles '--model opus' but misses '--model=opus', so a caller can receive both '--model haiku' and a caller-provided model flag. Normalize model flag detection before prepending the Haiku default.
acceptance_criteria: Daemon-managed Claude launch does not inject the default when args contain either '--model' or '--model=<value>'; tests cover both forms.
close_reason: Superseded by the v0 simulation cost policy: daemon/AOE launches now strip caller model args and pin Claude to haiku; tests cover both --model value and --model=value. Future adaptive model selection should be tracked separately when v0 simulations are complete.

title: Env-bound MCP bootstrap should prefer pinned peer id over launch id
description: src/mcp/tools/register.ts findEnvBoundPeer checks SYNCHRONIZE_LAUNCH_ID before SYNCHRONIZE_PEER_ID. Launched sessions receive both values, and peer_id is the durable pinned identity. If another binding exists for the same launch_id, bootstrap can activate the wrong peer before falling back to the pinned peer id.
acceptance_criteria: Env-bound MCP bootstrap filters by or prefers SYNCHRONIZE_PEER_ID when present; tests cover duplicate/foreign launch_id binding behavior.

title: Distribution: bundle Bun sidecar, sign + notarize .app (Phase 3 — v0 ship)
description: Ship a self-contained .app. Bundle the Bun runtime + daemon into .app/Contents/Resources as a Tauri sidecar; the shell spawns THAT daemon when none is running (works on a clean machine, no repo/global bun). Code sign + notarize (Developer ID). Optional: tauri-updater channel. This phase = v0 complete (single-window, no tabs).
acceptance_criteria: Distributable signed+notarized .app runs on a clean Mac with no checkout and no global bun; launches its own bundled daemon.

title: Native polish: notifications, dock badge, window state persistence (Phase 2)
description: Make it feel like a real Mac app: native notifications for new messages/mentions, dock unread badge, persist window size/position across launches, and deep-link from a notification to focus the right room. Builds on the working thin shell.
acceptance_criteria: New-message notification fires natively; dock badge reflects unread; window restores size/pos; clicking a notification focuses the relevant room.

title: Epic: native macOS desktop app (Tauri) reusing the web UI
description: Wrap the EXISTING synchronize web UI (web/) in a thin, native-feeling macOS shell using Tauri v2. The shell points its WKWebView at the local daemon's /web (load model A), supervises the daemon via a bundled Bun sidecar, and ships as a self-contained .app. v0 is single-window, NO tabs; tabs and mobile are deferred. Plan: docs/plans/macos-desktop-tauri.md. Decisions locked: load=point-at-external-daemon, tabs=in-app (deferred to last), packaging=Bun sidecar, one daemon shared by web+desktop, mobile=remote frontend (daemon on another device).
notes: Authored from planning + Plannotator review 2026-05-30. Reference: pingdotgg/t3code (Electron desktop loads backend httpBaseUrl; Expo/RN mobile reuses client-runtime+contracts). PENDING INDEX: when feat/web-chat-ui-saved merges to master, add docs/plans/macos-desktop-tauri.md to .claude/skills/synchronize-debugging/reference-v0-plans.md (Design docs table) — deferred because the index file is not on this branch.

title: Push parity for remote claude sessions (client-initiated SSE)
description: Restore live push to remote claude-mode sessions. Recommended option B: client opens long-lived SSE/long-poll outbound to the daemon (like /web/events) instead of daemon POSTing to a localhost callback (requireLocalCallbackUrl + 127.0.0.1 bind block this cross-machine). See plan Phase 2.

title: Verify remote claude-mode poll/inbox fallback across machines
description: Confirm remote claude sessions degrade gracefully (codex-mode polling / durable inbox) across the tailnet until push parity lands. End-to-end test. See plan Phase 1.5.

title: Web UI: bearer token entry for remote browsers
description: Capture ?token= query param into storage (or prompt) so a remote browser can authenticate to a tailnet-bound daemon without hand-editing localStorage SYNCHRONIZE_TOKEN. SSE already supports header auth via streaming fetch. See plan Phase 1.4.

title: Web UI: group sessions/peers by machine
description: Render 'this machine' vs other machines in the web UI using peers.machine_id (already NOT NULL in schema, surfaced in /web/state). Prefer Tailscale device name as stable key over os.hostname(). See plan Phase 1.3.

title: Web UI: Thread Summary panel + Kanban Board scaffolding

Scaffolding only — no backend wiring. Add a thin useThreadSummary seam (returns {text:null,status:'disabled'}) documenting the web-msg-id -> daemon root_event_id mapping for later integration with sync-b8q. Reuse existing CSS design tokens; additive/non-destructive changes only.
